trace.text:
NUNO_XI MD2 REASONING — Group Stage Round 2 (summer26) — REVISED Jun 18

FORMATION: 3-4-3 (budget 119/120)

CAPTAIN: Vinícius Júnior (BRA, FWD). Brazil vs Haiti is the biggest mismatch in MD2. Haiti ranked ~140th, no answer for Vinícius+Raphinha+Rodrygo who are all motivated after being held 1-1 by Morocco in MD1. Vinícius scored in MD1 and has a point to prove. Confirmed in Brazil's starting XI; attacking the space down Haiti's right is Brazil's most repeatable threat.

VICE-CAPTAIN: Mikel Oyarzabal (ESP, FWD). Spain must bounce back after a frustrating 0-0 vs Cabo Verde MD1 (2.26 xG, finishing failure not a system failure). Saudi Arabia are limited going forward. Oyarzabal is the penalty taker and Spain's most clinical finisher.

TRANSFERS (2 of 3 used, 1 saved for MD3):
1. Haaland OUT -> Vinícius Júnior IN: Brazil vs Haiti is a vastly better fixture than Norway vs Senegal (NOR win% collapsed 74->20 after MD1). Vinícius inherits the captaincy.
2. Raum OUT -> Hakimi IN: Raum only came on at 73' in MD1's blowout (1 pt). Hakimi takes penalties, free kicks and corners for Morocco, who held Brazil 1-1 in MD1 — they are not a pushover. Hakimi delivers real attacking output from RB plus triple set-piece EV vs Scotland.

FREE SWAP (REVISED): Fabián Ruiz INTO XI; Bruno Fernandes TO BENCH. Owner's call, and correct: Fabián arrives in the box to score and Spain vs Saudi Arabia (69% CS) is a strong fixture — he is confirmed in Spain's predicted XI. Bruno's penalty duty is contingent on Ronaldo being absent, so his goal-threat EV vs Uzbekistan is thin; he sits as bench cover. (Prior draft had this swap reversed — corrected before lock.)

3RD TRANSFER SAVED: Rolling to MD3 for knockout flexibility.

SPINE KEPT: Simón, Romero, Mendes, Wirtz, Musiala, Oyarzabal, Messi unchanged. MD1 delivered (Messi 14+, Haaland 13, Musiala 7, Romero 6, Simón 6). Don't break the spine over one bad game.

PREDICTIONS — DRAW CORRECTION: MD1 had 8/20 draws and we called zero. MD2 deliberately calls draws: MEX-KOR 1-1, TUR-PAR 1-1, NZL-EGY 1-1. PANAMA-CROATIA REVISED from 0-0 to 0-1 Croatia: Croatia are the stronger, more disciplined side and will chase the result they need; backing a clean shutout draw was too thin an edge — taking the correct-winner+diff EV instead.

MOROCCO NOTE: Morocco held Brazil 1-1 in MD1. We own Hakimi and predict Scotland 0-1 Morocco.
